name: scholar-sidekick-mcp
description: Resolve, format, and export academic citations from any AI assistant.
long_description: Scholar Sidekick MCP resolves identifiers (DOI, PMID, PMCID, ISBN, arXiv, ISSN, ADS bibcodes) into structured bibliographic metadata, formats citations in 10,000+ CSL styles (Vancouver, APA, AMA, IEEE, Chicago, Harvard, MLA, Nature, BMJ, Lancet, and many more), and exports references to BibTeX, RIS, CSL JSON, EndNote, RefWorks, MEDLINE, Zotero RDF, and CSV.

tools.name: resolveIdentifier
tools.title: Resolve Identifier
tools.description: Resolve one or more academic identifiers to structured bibliographic metadata in CSL JSON format. Use when the user wants raw reference data (title, authors, journal, year, DOI/PMID/ISBN, etc.) without formatting — e.g. to inspect, transform, or feed into another tool. Accepts DOI (with or without https://doi.org/), PMID (with or without 'PMID:' prefix), PMCID, ISBN (with or without hyphens), arXiv ID (with or without 'arXiv:' prefix), ISSN, and ADS bibcodes. Multiple identifiers can be passed in a single call separated by newlines or commas — batch when possible. Returns: a JSON array of CSL items, each with id, type, title, author (array of {family, given}), issued.date-parts, container-title (journal/book), DOI, PMID, PMCID, ISBN, ISSN, URL, and other CSL fields when available. Read-only; no side effects.

text.description: One or more identifiers to resolve (DOIs, PMIDs, PMCIDs, ISBNs, arXiv IDs, ISSNs, ADS bibcodes) separated by newlines or commas

items.description: Array of resolved bibliographic items in CSL JSON format

type.description: CSL item type (e.g. article-journal, book, chapter)

issued.description: Publication date in CSL date format

tools.name: formatCitation
tools.title: Format Citation
tools.description: Format one or more academic identifiers into a human-readable citation in a specific style. Use when the user wants a finished citation string they can paste into a manuscript, slide, or message — e.g. 'format this DOI in APA' or 'give me a Vancouver citation for PMID:12345'. Accepts the same identifier formats as resolveIdentifier (DOI, PMID, PMCID, ISBN, arXiv, ISSN, ADS bibcode), with or without prefixes. Style defaults to Vancouver if not specified — ask the user before defaulting if any ambiguity exists. Supports Vancouver, APA, AMA, IEEE, CSE, Chicago (author-date and notes), Harvard, MLA, Turabian, Nature, BMJ, Lancet, and any of 10,000+ CSL style IDs from the citation-style-language/styles repository. Output defaults to plain text; pass output=html for marked-up HTML or output=json for the structured CSL items. Returns: { text? | html? | items?, styleUsed, formatter, warnings? } — exactly one of text, html, or items will be populated based on the output parameter, plus the resolved style ID and any non-fatal warnings (missing fields, locale fallbacks). Read-only; no side effects.

text.description: One or more identifiers (DOIs, PMIDs, ISBNs, arXiv IDs, etc.) separated by newlines or commas

style.description: Citation style: vancouver (default), ama, apa, ieee, cse, or any CSL style ID

lang.description: Locale for formatting (e.g. en-US, en-GB, fr-FR)

footnote.description: Format as footnotes instead of bibliography entries

outputSchema.description: Formatted citation output. Exactly one of 'text', 'html', or 'items' will be present depending on the requested 'output' format.

text.description: Formatted citation as plain text (when output=text)

styleUsed.description: The citation style ID actually applied

formatter.description: Backend formatter used to produce the output

warnings.description: Any non-fatal warnings (e.g. missing fields, locale fallback)

tools.name: exportCitation
tools.title: Export Citation
tools.description: Export one or more academic identifiers as a bibliography file in a chosen reference-manager format. Use when the user wants a file they can import into Zotero, Mendeley, EndNote, RefWorks, LaTeX (BibTeX), Excel (CSV), or similar — e.g. 'give me these as BibTeX' or 'export to RIS'. Format parameter is required: 'bib' (BibTeX), 'ris' (RIS), 'csl' (CSL JSON), 'endnote-xml' (EndNote XML), 'endnote-refer' (EndNote Refer/tagged), 'refworks' (RefWorks tagged), 'medline' (NBIB/MEDLINE), 'zotero-rdf' (Zotero RDF), 'csv' (spreadsheet-friendly), or 'txt' (plain-text bibliography rendered with the optional style parameter). Accepts the same identifier formats as resolveIdentifier. Returns: a single string containing the entire bibliography in the requested format, ready to be saved to a file (.bib, .ris, .csv, .xml, .nbib, .rdf, etc.) or pasted into the target tool. Read-only; no side effects.

text.description: One or more identifiers (DOIs, PMIDs, ISBNs, etc.) separated by newlines or commas

style.description: Citation style (used only for txt export)

content.description: Exported bibliography as a string in the requested format (BibTeX, RIS, CSL JSON, EndNote, RefWorks, MEDLINE, Zotero RDF, CSV, or plain text)

prompts.name: format
prompts.description: Format an academic identifier in a specific citation style.

arguments.name: identifier
arguments.description: Identifier to format (DOI, PMID, PMCID, ISBN, arXiv ID, ISSN, ADS bibcode)

arguments.name: style
arguments.description: Citation style: vancouver, apa, ama, ieee, cse, or any CSL style ID

prompts.name: export
prompts.description: Resolve an identifier and export it to a bibliography file format.

arguments.name: identifier
arguments.description: Identifier to export (DOI, PMID, ISBN, arXiv ID, etc.)

arguments.name: format
arguments.description: Export format: bib, ris, csv, csl, endnote-xml, endnote-refer, refworks, medline, zotero-rdf, txt

prompts.name: batch_format
prompts.description: Format multiple identifiers as a bibliography in a single style.

arguments.name: identifiers
arguments.description: Identifiers separated by newlines or commas

arguments.name: style
arguments.description: Citation style to apply to all entries

prompts.name: resolve
prompts.description: Resolve an identifier to structured bibliographic metadata.

arguments.name: identifier
arguments.description: Identifier to resolve (DOI, PMID, PMCID, ISBN, arXiv ID, ISSN, ADS bibcode)

prompts.text: Resolve ${arguments.identifier} and return the bibliographic metadata as structured JSON.

rapidapi_key.title: RapidAPI Key
rapidapi_key.description: Your RapidAPI subscription key for Scholar Sidekick. Optional — the server will install and start without one, but every tool call returns a configuration message until a key is set. Get a free key at https://rapidapi.com/scholar-sidekick-scholar-sidekick-api/api/scholar-sidekick

rapidapi_host.title: RapidAPI Host (advanced)
rapidapi_host.description: Override the default RapidAPI host. Leave blank unless instructed.

timeout_ms.title: Request timeout (ms)
timeout_ms.description: How long to wait for the Scholar Sidekick API before failing. Default 30000.
